disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Widespread pruritic red-brown lesions that urticate on friction (Darier's sign), flushing, and deep bone pain
trigger_question_examples: What does the rash look like and does rubbing it bring up hives?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Red-brown itchy spots that hive when rubbed, flushing, and deep thigh bone pain.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No breathing difficulty/wheeze/throat tightness/voice change (airway/breathing screen)
trigger_question_examples: Any trouble breathing, wheeze, throat tightness, or voice change?
Does your throat feel like it's closing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — I can breathe and swallow fine, my voice is normal.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No dizziness/faintness on standing, no collapse (circulation screen)
trigger_question_examples: Do you feel faint/dizzy, especially standing?
Have you felt you might pass out?

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: In-date adrenaline autoinjectors available; recurrent-anaphylaxis history
trigger_question_examples: Do you have your adrenaline pens, and are they in date?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, my EpiPens are here and in date, and I've had anaphylaxis before.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Widespread urticating red-brown maculopapular lesions with positive Darier's sign; patent airway/clear chest; long-bone ache (examination)
trigger_question_examples: (requires airway/chest exam and Darier's sign not available over chat)
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: (airway/chest exam and Darier's sign need in-person assessment; the anaphylaxis symptom screen is the remote proxy)

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is my throat going to close? Do I need an ambulance or an injection?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Take anaphylaxis seriously and screen breathing/throat/circulation/GI. If any are present, advise adrenaline (she has pens) and 000 immediately. If it is skin/bone only, reassure it is a cutaneous/skeletal flare, advise H1 antihistamine up-dosing, an H2 adjunct and cooling, confirm the adrenaline pens are ready, and give a very low-threshold anaphylaxis safety-net. Do not arrange a remote injection or over-escalate an isolated flare.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: The one antihistamine didn't help — what can I take?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Advise up-dosing the non-sedating H1 antihistamine (as used for mast cell disease), adding an H2 blocker, cooling, avoiding NSAIDs (a degranulation trigger), and loose clothing. Do not offer injections. Reinforce the anaphylaxis safety-net.

deflection_behaviours.description: Airway anxiety may prompt demands for an ambulance/injection before the systemic screen is done; tests whether the AI Doctor screens anaphylaxis and responds proportionately.
deflection_behaviours.deflection_text_template: I'm too scared, just send an ambulance / give me an injection...
